{"step_by_step_thinking": "The external carotid artery has several branches, including the posterior auricular artery, occipital artery, and maxillary artery. These are all well-documented branches of the external carotid artery. The ophthalmic artery, however, is a branch of the internal carotid artery, not the external carotid artery. This is confirmed by multiple sources, including the MEDCORP document which explicitly states that the ophthalmic artery is the first branch of the internal carotid artery, originating distal to the cavernous sinus. Therefore, the ophthalmic artery is not a branch of the external carotid artery. Thus, option D is incorrect as a branch of the external carotid artery.", "answer_choice": "D"}
